mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative or flat when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hikes, producing positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 25 basis points per month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a positive or zero correlation with CPIAUCSL increases over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC > 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 300,000 signals an overheating labor market that precedes wage-driven inflation and eventual monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 300,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a shift in consumer spending toward durable assets, which compresses near-term corporate profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations where HOUST rises while RSAFS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO declines during the specified recovery regime.
